slides.title: SvelteKit & Elysia Mastery
slides.subtitle:
The Ultimate Full-Stack Developer Experience
From Reactive Runes to Type-Safe Elysia APIs

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Full-Stack Developer
slides.experience: Specialist in Reactive Frameworks & High-Performance APIs
slides.oss_experience: Open Source Contributor & Core Contributor to Reactive Tooling

list.text: Predictable: Structure clearly maps to URLs.
Dynamic: Folders with [brackets] handle dynamic parameters.

box.title: SvelteKit uses a directory-based router where folders define routes and files like +page.svelte define content.
Fetch data before the page renders, supporting both server-side and client-side execution.
The standard way to handle form submissions without manual fetch calls, using native HTML forms with progressive enhancement.

list.text: Flexibility: Build once, deploy to any cloud provider.
Edge: Optimized for edge runtimes with adapter-cloudflare.

box.title: SvelteKit is runtime-agnostic through its adapter system (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare, Node).

list.text: Signal-based: Efficient fine-grained updates.
Simplified: Use reactivity anywhere, not just in .svelte files.

box.title: Runes are a set of primitives that provide universal reactivity, replacing top-level variables and stores in many cases.

box.code:
let count = $state(0);
const increment = () => count += 1;
box.title: Declares reactive state in a way that is modern, explicit, and highly performant.

box.code:
let count = $state(0);
let doubled = $derived(count * 2);
box.title: Computed values that automatically update when their dependencies change.
Handles side effects, replacing the old $: syntax with more control and better performance.

box.code: let { name, age } = $props();
box.title: The new way to define component props with full TypeScript support and cleaner syntax.

list.text: Fast: Outperforms Express and Fastify in Bun.
Safe: Built-in end-to-end type safety.

box.title: A high-performance Bun-native framework focusing on developer experience and type safety.

list.text: Efficient: Predictable performance at scale.
Swagger: Instant documentation with easy-to-use plugins.

box.title: Elysia uses static analysis to optimize route execution and generate OpenAPI schemas automatically.
A unified object containing request, response, set (headers/status), and more.

box.code: app.get('/video', () => Bun.file('movie.mp4'));
box.title: Elysia leverages Bun's native features like `Bun.file` and `Bun.serve` for maximum throughput.

list.text: Seamless: Shares types directly from your Elysia server.
Zero-Config: No need for tRPC-style boilerplate.

box.title: A high-performance client for Elysia that provides full type safety without code generation or heavy dependencies.
The standard client that allows you to call your API using object property access, with full IDE autocompletion.

box.code:
const fetch = edenFetch<App>('http://localhost:3000');
const data = await fetch('/user/:id', { params: { id: '1' } });
box.title: A lightweight fetch wrapper that provides type safety for standard fetch-like calls.

list.text: Reliable: If the schema changes, the client type changes instantly.
Safety: Prevents sending invalid data to the server.

box.title: Eden automatically extracts validation schemas (Zod, TypeBox) and reflects them in the client types.

list.text: SSR Loaders: Fetch initial data from Elysia during server-side render.
Hydration: Eden client takes over for interactive client-side updates.

box.title: Strategy for combining SvelteKit's SSR with Elysia's high-speed API logic.
Best practices for proxying SvelteKit form actions to an Elysia backend for specialized processing.

list.text: Low Latency: Compute happens near the user.
Unified Runtime: Shared Bun environment simplifies configuration.

box.title: Running both SvelteKit and Elysia on the edge (e.g., Cloudflare Workers) for global performance.
Using Eden for reactive WebSocket or SSE connections that update Svelte state in real-time.

box.code:
const plugin = new Elysia().get('/p', () => 'Ping');
const app = new Elysia().use(plugin);
box.title: Elysia follows a 'composable' plugin model where any instance can be used as a plugin for another instance.
Hook into request execution at various stages: `onRequest`, `beforeHandle`, `afterHandle`, `onResponse`, and `onError`.

list.text: Swagger: Interactive API documentation.
JWT: Token-based authentication.
CORS: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing.

box.title: Elysia provides a rich set of official plugins for common tasks.

box.code:
const auth = (app) => app.onBeforeHandle(...) 
app.use(auth);
box.title: Creating reusable plugins is easy with the `.use()` method and type inference.

box.code:
import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
export const theme = writable('light');
box.title: A look at the traditional `writable`, `readable`, and `derived` stores for global state management.

list.text: Intuitive: Regular classes can now be reactive.
Decoupled: Better separation of concerns.

box.title: How Runes simplify global state and cross-component communication without explicit stores.

box.code:
<form use:enhance={(...) => {
  // Preliminary state update
  return async ({ update }) => { ... };
}}>
box.title: Implementing smooth UX by updating the UI before the server responds, using SvelteKit's `enhance` action.

list.text: Socket to Rune: Direct mapping of event data to $state.
Feedback: User actions reflected immediately across all clients.

box.title: Connecting Elysia's real-time events to Svelte's reactive variables for instant feedback loops.

list.text: PostCSS/SCSS: Advanced styling techniques.
Custom Logic: Injecting code or transforming markup at build time.

box.title: Customizing the compilation step. How to use tools like `svelte-preprocess` or `vite-plugin-svelte` for specialized syntax.
Define custom route properties that can inject logic and metadata automatically, improving code reuse and safety.

list.text: Deep Types: Navigating complex API responses with ease.
Strictness: Catching subtle type mismatches during development.

box.title: Pushing TypeScript to the limit. Inferring complex nested structures and union types across the full-stack boundary.

box.code: Hover me
box.title: Extending Svelte's capabilities with custom `use:action` directives for DOM manipulation or third-party library integration.
The standard choice for Bun-first apps: Type-safe, lightweight, and focused on predictable SQL performance.

list.text: Global Replication: Data stays close to your compute.
Scale: Handle millions of databases with ease.

box.title: A distributed SQLite database built on LibSQL, perfect for low-latency global applications.

box.code:
# shell
bun drizzle-kit generate:sqlite
box.title: Using Drizzle Kit to generate and push migrations, staying in sync with your TypeScript schemas.
Connecting database updates to Svelte state. Using SvelteKit's `invalidate` or `invalidateAll` to refresh data from Elysia.

box.code: bun index.ts // Servant both SvelteKit and Elysia
box.title: Running the entire stack on a single Bun instance for maximum simplicity and performance.

list.text: Pages: Host the SvelteKit frontend.
Workers: Run the Elysia API.

box.title: Leveraging Cloudflare's edge infrastructure. Elysia handles the backend logic while SvelteKit manages the frontend.

box.code:
FROM oven/bun:latest
COPY . .
CMD ["bun", "src/index.ts"]
box.title: Creating a lightweight Bun-based Docker image for consistent deployment across any cloud provider.

list.text: Optimized: Each part is hosted where it performs best.
Scalable: Scale your API independently of your frontend.

box.title: Hosting the frontend on a CDN (Vercel/Netlify) and the Elysia API on a dedicated VPS or serverless platform.
Fast, Vite-native unit testing for Svelte components and logic with full Rune support.

box.code:
const res = await app.handle(new Request('http://localhost/'));
expect(await res.text()).toBe('Hello');
box.title: Isolated testing of Elysia routes by directly calling the `app.handle()` method with simulated requests.

list.text: Type-safe tests: Catch API breaks at compile time.
Reliability: Tests and code stay perfectly in sync.

box.title: Ensuring that your tests reflect the same type safety as your production code via Eden.

box.code:
await page.goto('/');
await expect(page.locator('h1')).toHaveText('Welcome');
box.title: Automating real user interactions across the entire stack to ensure seamless integration.

list.text: Speed: Bun-native performance everywhere.
Safety: End-to-end type safety with Eden.
Experience: Reactive UI with a minimalist API.

box.title: SvelteKit and Elysia represent a powerful, type-safe, and high-performance combination for modern web apps.

list.text: Runes: The future of reactive programming.
Tooling: Faster builds and better DX with Vite.

box.title: Keep an eye on Svelte 5 stable release, Elysia 1.0+ core improvements, and the growth of the Bun ecosystem.
Master the runes. Bridge the stack with Eden. Build the next generation of web apps.
Thank you! Any questions?
